report_name: Ransomware Market Consolidation: Qilin, Akira, and DragonForce Dominate March 2026 Attacks

threat_actors.name: Qilin
threat_actors.description: A well-established Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) operation active since 2022, identified as the most prolific group in March 2026, accounting for 20% of all publicly claimed attacks.

threat_actors.name: Akira
threat_actors.description: A successful Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) group that accounted for 12% of attacks in March 2026, doubling its activity from the previous month and heavily targeting the business services and industrial manufacturing sectors.

threat_actors.name: DragonForce
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group operating a 'cartel' model, responsible for 8% of attacks in March 2026. Its recent surge is attributed to absorbing affiliates from the defunct RansomHub operation.

threat_actors.name: RansomHub
threat_actors.description: A defunct ransomware operation whose affiliates were reportedly absorbed by the DragonForce group.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Used by the dominant ransomware groups for initial access by exploiting unpatched vulnerabilities in public-facing services like VPNs and RDP.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Used as a primary initial access vector by the dominant ransomware groups to steal credentials.

attack_patterns.name: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: Used by the ransomware groups for lateral movement and execution as part of a 'Living off the Land' strategy.

attack_patterns.name: Service Execution
attack_patterns.description: A common technique used by the top ransomware groups for execution and lateral movement.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: Used by the ransomware groups to exfiltrate sensitive data as part of their double extortion strategy. The article incorrectly references T1041 as 'Exfiltrate Data to Cloud Storage'.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The primary impact technique used by the ransomware groups to encrypt files on the victim's network for extortion.

attack_patterns.name: LSASS Memory
attack_patterns.description: Implied as a technique used by the ransomware groups, as the report suggests behavioral detection for unusual processes accessing lsass.exe memory.

malware.name: Qilin Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload used by the Qilin RaaS operation, which was the most active in March 2026.

malware.name: Akira Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload used by the Akira RaaS group, which accounted for 12% of attacks in March 2026.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: A legitimate tool used by the ransomware groups for lateral movement and execution.

identities.name: Business Services Sector
identities.description: A high-value sector heavily targeted by ransomware groups like Akira in March 2026.

identities.name: Industrial Manufacturing Sector
identities.description: A high-value sector heavily targeted by ransomware groups like Akira in March 2026.

identities.name: Consumer Goods Sector
identities.description: A sector identified as being at clear and present danger from ransomware attacks.

infrastructure.name: DragonForce Shared Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Infrastructure provided by the DragonForce cartel for its affiliates to use in attacks.

indicators.name: Suspicious LSASS Memory Access
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious LSASS Memory Access For Credential Dumping
id: 2a1b3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects an unusual process accessing the memory of lsass.exe, which is a common TTP for credential dumping.
references:
  - https://www.digit.fyi/three-ransomware-gangs-behind-40-of-march-attacks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-13
tags:
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.credential_access
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_access
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetImage|endswith: '\lsass.exe'
  filter:
    SourceImage:
      - 'C:\Windows\System32\svchost.exe'
      - 'C:\Windows\System32\wininit.exe'
      - 'C:\Windows\System32\services.exe'
      - 'C:\Windows\System32\csrss.exe'
      - 'C:\Windows\System32\smss.exe'
      - 'C:\Windows\System32\wbem\WmiPrvSE.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects behavioral anomalies where an unusual process accesses the memory of lsass.exe, a technique used for credential dumping by ransomware groups.

indicators.name: PowerShell Execution from Office Application
indicators.pattern:
title: PowerShell Execution from Microsoft Office
id: 6f5e4d3c-2b1a-4f7e-8d9c-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects PowerShell being spawned from a Microsoft Office application, which is a common pattern for macro-based attacks and initial execution.
references:
  - https://www.digit.fyi/three-ransomware-gangs-behind-40-of-march-attacks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-13
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects suspicious process chains where powershell.exe is spawned from a Microsoft Office application, a common TTP for initial execution.
